title: Search an element in an array

content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of size NNN and an element XXX. Your task is to find whether the array AAA contains the element XXX or not.


Input Format

The first line contains two space-separated integers NNN and XXX — the size of array and the element to be searched.
The second line contains all the elements of array AAA


Output Format
Output "YES" if the element XXX is present in AAA, otherwise output "NO".
constraints: The sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 10^5
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10) — the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines:
- The first line contains two space‑separated integers N and X (1 ≤ N ≤ 10^5) — the size of the array and the element to search for.
- The second line contains N space‑separated integers A_i (1 ≤ A_i ≤ 10^5) — the elements of the array.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if X appears in the corresponding array, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: There is a single test case. The array contains the element 3, therefore the answer is YES.
The element 10 does not appear in the array, so the output is NO.
The only element of the array equals X, thus the answer is YES.

solution.approach: For each test case we scan the array once and keep a boolean flag that becomes true as soon as we encounter an element equal to X. After reading all N numbers we output "YES" if the flag is true, otherwise "NO". This is the optimal solution because any algorithm must at least look at each element to be certain of the answer.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the size of the array. Over all test cases the total time is O(∑N) ≤ O(10^5).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) additional space besides the input storage.
